mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations. A month-over-month drop in FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points over a single month, S&P 500 returns turn negative within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of stable unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand and credit availability. When RSAFS and HOUST both register positive month-over-month growth, S&P 500 forward returns are positive over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (accuracy < 52%), or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production reflects supply-side bottlenecks and falling business investment. During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month decline in INDPRO predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in >58% of cases, or the predictive relationship reverses sign.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in headline inflation reduces near-term rate hike expectations and boosts equity risk premiums. When CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth drops below 0.2%, S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL mom falls below 0.2%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining total nonfarm payrolls indicates weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate revenue growth. A month-over-month decrease in PAYEMS predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in >55% of instances, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and retail sales signals shifting demand composition. When RSAFS growth outpaces GDPC1 growth by more than 1.5 percentage points, consumer-driven sectors lift the S&P 500, producing positive returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The RSAFS-GDPC1 spread fails to predict positive 20-day returns (accuracy < 51%), or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contractions in industrial output and housing starts signal a broad economic slowdown. During recessionary periods, when both INDPRO and HOUST decline month-over-month, S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in INDPRO and HOUST during recessions are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in >60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power outside recessionary windows.
